objective.description: Maintain a scalable, predictable, and domain-oriented frontend architecture. Keep route definitions thin, group business logic by domain, and enforce clear dependency boundaries.

core_principles.principle: The app directory is responsible for routing, layouts, metadata, loading states, error boundaries, and page composition.
Business logic must be organized by business domain under the domains directory.
Shared components must contain only domain-independent and reusable UI elements.
Route-specific code should remain colocated near the route when it is not reused elsewhere.
Server Components are the default. Client Components must be introduced only when browser APIs, React state, effects, event handlers, or client-side libraries are required.
Shared infrastructure code belongs in lib. Domain-specific API functions and business rules belong in the corresponding domain.
Avoid premature abstraction. Create new layers or folders only when responsibilities become clearly separated.

directory_structure.tree: src/ ├── app/ │ ├── (auth)/ │ │ ├── login/ │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ ├── callback/ │ │ │ ├── auth-callback-client.tsx │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ └── layout.tsx │ │ │ ├── (dashboard)/ │ │ ├── dashboard/ │ │ │ ├── contis/ │ │ │ │ ├── [id]/ │ │ │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ │ │ ├── new/ │ │ │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ │ ├── songs/ │ │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ │ ├── teams/ │ │ │ │ ├── create/ │ │ │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ │ ├── settings/ │ │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ └── layout.tsx │ │ │ ├── share/ │ │ └── contis/ │ │ └── [token]/ │ │ └── page.tsx │ │ │ ├── globals.css │ ├── layout.tsx │ └── page.tsx │ ├── domains/ │ ├── auth/ │ │ ├── api/ │ │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── hooks/ │ │ ├── models/ │ │ └── index.ts │ │ │ ├── dashboard/ │ │ ├── api/ │ │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── hooks/ │ │ ├── models/ │ │ └── index.ts │ │ │ ├── team/ │ │ ├── api/ │ │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── hooks/ │ │ ├── models/ │ │ ├── utils/ │ │ └── index.ts │ │ │ ├── song/ │ │ ├── api/ │ │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── hooks/ │ │ ├── models/ │ │ └── utils/ │ │ │ └── conti/ │ ├── api/ │ ├── components/ │ ├── hooks/ │ ├── models/ │ └── utils/ │ ├── components/ │ ├── ui/ │ ├── auth/ │ ├── analytics/ │ ├── providers/ │ ├── sections/ │ └── layout/ │ ├── lib/ │ ├── api/ │ ├── mock/ │ ├── api.ts │ ├── analytics.js │ ├── safe-url.ts │ ├── toast.ts │ └── utils.ts │ ├── context/ ├── hooks/ ├── stores/ ├── types/ ├── constants/ └── middleware.ts

rules.rule: Keep page.tsx and layout.tsx thin.
Do not place complex business logic directly inside page.tsx.
Do not place reusable domain components directly under app.
Use route groups such as (auth), (dashboard), or (admin) when routes require different layouts or conceptual grouping.
Use private folders such as _components, _hooks, or _lib for route-specific code that is not reused outside the route.
Organize code by business capability, not only by technical type.
Each domain must own its internal implementation.
External modules should import from the domain public API when possible.
Avoid importing internal files from another domain.
Do not move a component to the global components directory unless it is genuinely domain-independent.

recommended_internal_structure.folder: API request functions, DTO definitions, and response mappers.
UI components used only within the domain.
Domain-specific React hooks and query hooks.
Frontend domain models and view models.
Optional Zod, Valibot, or other runtime validation schemas.
Optional domain-local TypeScript types when models are not the right name.
Domain-specific helper functions and transformation logic.
recommended_internal_structure.file: Public API of the domain. Export only stable modules intended for use outside the domain. Existing large domains may add this gradually instead of creating broad barrels.

rules.rule: Shared UI primitives under components/ui must not depend on a specific business domain.
App-shell components under components/layout, components/auth, components/providers, or components/analytics may depend on domains when they compose application-wide behavior.
Domain-aware reusable UI belongs in domains/{domain}/components, not components/ui.
Prefer composition through props instead of hard-coded business rules.
lib contains infrastructure and cross-cutting concerns.
Do not place domain-specific business logic in lib.
API client configuration belongs in src/lib/api.ts or src/lib/api.
Concrete domain API calls belong in domains/{domain}/api.
Existing src/lib/api/{domain}.ts files are compatibility re-exports only. Do not add new business logic there.

responsibilities.item: Hooks independent of a specific business domain

rules.rule: A hook used only by one domain must remain inside that domain.

responsibilities.item: Application-wide Zustand, Redux, or equivalent stores

rules.rule: Prefer server state tools for server-owned data.
Do not place all remote API data into a global client store.
Domain-local client state should remain inside the domain unless multiple independent domains require it.
Domain-specific types should remain inside the domain.
Existing src/types/{domain}.ts files may act as compatibility aliases for older imports, but new domain models and DTOs should be defined under domains/{domain}.
Do not turn the global types directory into a dumping ground.

placement_decision_rules.rule: If code is used only by one route, place it under that route using a private folder.
If code is reused across multiple routes within one domain, place it inside domains/{domain}.
If code is reusable across multiple unrelated domains and has no business meaning, place it under components, hooks, lib, types, or constants as appropriate.
If code represents a business concept, keep it inside the owning domain even when more than one page uses it.
Do not promote code to a global directory solely because it is used twice. Promote it only when the abstraction is stable and domain-independent.

dependency_rules.allowed_direction:
app ├──> domains ├──> components ├──> context ├──> hooks ├──> stores └──> lib
domains ├──> components ├──> lib ├──> hooks ├──> constants ├──> context └──> shared types
components ├──> lib ├──> hooks ├──> constants └──> shared types
lib ├──> stores ├──> constants └──> shared types

forbidden_dependencies.dependency: shared UI primitives in components/ui must not import from domains.
lib must not import from app.
lib must not contain dependencies on a specific business domain, except temporary src/lib/api/{domain}.ts compatibility re-exports.
one domain must not import another domain's private internal modules.
lower-level shared modules must not depend on higher-level route modules.

domain_interaction.rule: When one domain needs another domain, prefer importing from the target domain's public API.
When a target domain has no public API yet, direct imports are allowed temporarily if the dependency is explicit, narrow, and not duplicated elsewhere.
If two domains depend heavily on each other, reconsider their domain boundaries.
Shared business concepts may be extracted into a separate domain or shared domain module only when ownership is clear.

server_client_component_rules.default: All components are Server Components unless client behavior is explicitly required.

use_server_component_when.condition: Keeping sensitive logic out of the browser bundle

use_client_component_when.condition: Using useState, useReducer, useEffect, or browser event handlers
Using browser APIs such as localStorage or window

rules.rule: Place "use client" at the lowest practical component boundary.
Do not mark an entire page or layout as a Client Component when only a small child component requires interactivity.
Server-only modules should import server-only when appropriate.
Do not import server-only modules into Client Components.

page_file.name: page.tsx

responsibilities.item: Read route params and search params

restrictions.item: No direct global state orchestration unless necessary

page_file.migration_note: Existing large client pages such as dashboard/songs and dashboard/contis/new may be refactored gradually. New work should move reusable form sections, mutation orchestration, and domain workflows into domains/{domain}/components or domains/{domain}/hooks instead of growing page.tsx further.

responsibilities.item: Provider placement at the narrowest valid boundary
Temporary re-export compatibility for older imports

compatibility_exports.rule: Do not add new endpoint implementation or business logic to compatibility exports.

rules.rule: Do not call raw fetch or axios repeatedly across UI components.
UI components should call domain-level functions or hooks.
Keep transport DTOs separate from frontend domain models when their shapes differ.
Convert snake_case backend fields into frontend conventions at the API boundary.

categories.category: Data sent to an external API.
Raw data returned by an external API.
Frontend representation of a business concept.
Data shaped specifically for a UI component or page.

rules.rule: Do not automatically use raw response DTOs directly in UI components.
Create mapping functions when backend and frontend representations differ.
Keep domain-specific request, response, domain, and view-model types inside the owning domain.

server_state.rule: Server-owned data should not be duplicated into a global client store without necessity.

client_state.rule: Keep state local until multiple distant components require shared ownership.

global_state.rule: Use global stores only for genuinely cross-cutting client state.
Separate stores by responsibility rather than creating one monolithic store.

directories.rule: Use lowercase kebab-case for route segments.
Use lowercase singular or domain-consistent names for domain directories.

components.rule: Use kebab-case filenames for components and PascalCase exported component names.

api_files.rule: Use domain-scoped API module names for grouped endpoint modules.

stores.rule: Use a clear store suffix such as -store.ts.

server_files.rule: Use the .server.ts suffix when distinguishing server-only modules improves clarity.

rules.rule: Use index.ts only at meaningful module boundaries.
Do not create barrel files in every directory.
Export only modules intended for external consumption.
Keep internal implementation files private to the domain.
Avoid circular dependencies caused by broad barrel exports.

testing_structure.examples:
tests/ ├── conti-editor.test.mjs ├── song-library.test.mjs └── team-permissions.test.mjs
src/domains/ └── song/ ├── components/ │ ├── song-detail-dialog.tsx │ └── song-detail-dialog.test.tsx ├── api/ │ ├── song.mapper.ts │ └── song.mapper.test.ts └── utils/ ├── song-library.js └── song-library.test.mjs

rules.rule: Existing top-level tests may remain under tests while they cover stable behavior.
New TypeScript unit tests may be colocated near the implementation when the test tooling supports it.
End-to-end tests may live in a top-level e2e directory.
Test domain behavior rather than internal implementation details.

prohibited_patterns.pattern: Putting all components from every domain into one global components directory.
Putting all API calls into one large api.ts file.
Putting all TypeScript types into one global types.ts file.
Writing complex business logic directly in page.tsx.
Marking large route trees as client components without necessity.
Importing another domain's internal component, hook, or utility directly when a public API exists.
Using a global state store for all server-fetched data.
Moving code to shared directories before the abstraction is stable.
Creating generic utility modules that contain unrelated functions.
Mixing transport DTOs, domain models, and UI-specific models without distinction.

agent_decision_process.step: Identify whether the code belongs to routing, a business domain, shared UI, infrastructure, or application-wide configuration.
Determine the narrowest scope in which the code is used: one route, one domain, multiple domains, or the whole application.
Place the code in the narrowest appropriate directory.
Verify that the proposed dependency direction follows the architecture rules.
Prefer a Server Component unless client behavior is required.
Separate API transport types from domain and view models when necessary.
Avoid creating new abstractions unless they remove duplication without hiding business meaning.
Check that shared modules do not depend on domain-specific code.

agent_output_requirements.requirement: When creating a new file, state the selected path and why it belongs there.
When moving a file, verify all affected imports and dependency boundaries.
When introducing a Client Component, explain which client-only capability requires it.
When creating a shared abstraction, confirm that it is used across unrelated domains and contains no domain-specific business rules.
When adding a domain, follow the existing internal structure instead of creating a new global technical folder.
Do not modify the architecture convention without explicitly documenting the reason.

example.name: thin_page
shared_api_client
domain_api
domain_public_api
route_specific_component
example.reason: This component is specific to the callback route and does not need to be part of the reusable auth domain API unless another route starts using it.

example.name: domain_component
example.reason: This component represents team-domain behavior and should remain inside the team domain unless it becomes domain-independent.

example.name: shared_ui_component
example.reason: This component has no business-domain knowledge and can be reused across unrelated domains.

nextjs_frontend_architecture.final_rule: Prefer explicit ownership, narrow scope, and one-way dependency flow over convenience-driven file placement.
